title: Agents and Infrastructure, Sam Lambert | Compile 26
summary: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, discusses how database infrastructure must evolve to support AI agents. He demonstrates agents autonomously optimizing and sharding a production database while maintaining safety through branching, rollback capabilities, and controlled feedback loops.
writeup: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, argues that database infrastructure has to change to accommodate AI agents acting on production systems. The talk includes a live demonstration of agents autonomously optimizing and sharding a production database, with safety preserved through branching, rollback, and controlled feedback loops. The core point: agent autonomy over databases depends on guardrails that let changes be tested and reversed rather than applied blindly.
